prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='rf', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.538) and substantial seasonality variance (0.7341), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, while early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists. These conditions strongly support dba_combination with mixed weighting to leverage diverse model behaviors without over-relying on any single approach.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_DWT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_CWT_svr", "late": "DWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_rf", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.538,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.538) and substantial seasonality variance (0.7341), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, while early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists. These conditions strongly support dba_combination with mixed weighting to leverage diverse model behaviors without over-relying on any single approach."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model diversity across horizons, supporting per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.178 shows moderate disagreement but rmse_spread_ratio=0.538 suggests substantial model dispersion. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts and non-linear divergence (seasonality_corr_variance=0.7341). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate to avoid instability. The combination of DBA (handles phase shifts), topk_mean (captures horizon specialists), inverse_rmse_weights (leverages performance differences), and best_per_horizon (exploits 8 unique winners) provides robust coverage."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), and 1 baseline (dba_combination). With n_windows=3, the set is diverse enough. No leakage detected - all candidates use proper anti-leakage evaluation. The universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim1.0 as RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE leader but current weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 (score 0.879) outperforms it. No pruning needed - set has 4 candidates with different types, no redundancy detected.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.538) and substantial seasonality variance (0.7341), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, while early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists. These conditions strongly support dba_combination with mixed weighting to leverage diverse model behaviors without over-relying on any single approach.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model diversity across horizons, supporting per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.178 shows moderate disagreement but rmse_spread_ratio=0.538 suggests substantial model dispersion. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts and non-linear divergence (seasonality_corr_variance=0.7341). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate to avoid instability. The combination of DBA (handles phase shifts), topk_mean (captures horizon specialists), inverse_rmse_weights (leverages performance differences), and best_per_horizon (exploits 8 unique winners) provides robust coverage.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), and 1 baseline (dba_combination). With n_windows=3, the set is diverse enough. No leakage detected - all candidates use proper anti-leakage evaluation. The universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim1.0 as RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE leader but current weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 (score 0.879) outperforms it. No pruning needed - set has 4 candidates with different types, no redundancy detected.
